headline: Airbus, Boeing fly in different directions at Farnborough
summary: Airbus and Boeing presented differing outlooks for their commercial aircraft in coming years, as the aviation sector gathered near London for this week's Farnborough airshow. "From a financial perspective, it is driven by production rates," Stephanie Pope, head of Boeing Commercial Airplanes, said at Farnborough.

headline: Boeing beats Airbus with 173 plane orders at Farnborough Airshow
summary: Investing.com -- Boeing secured 173 orders at the Farnborough Airshow this week, edging ahead of Airbus which booked 154 jets, as planemakers continue to navigate ongoing supply chain constraints and work through record order backlogs.

headline: IBM buys HRL Laboratories in shift to two-track quantum computing strategy
summary: IBM has agreed to buy HRL Laboratories, a private quantum computing research lab jointly owned by Boeing Co and General Motors, the ‌company said on Thursday, adding a second pillar to IBM's quantum computing efforts.  IBM has been racing ‌against Alphabet's Google, Microsoft and others to create practical quantum machines that can crack problems that would take conventional computers thousands ​of years to solve.  In May, U.S. President Donald Trump's administration said it would award $1 billion to IBM to set up a new company called Anderon in New Albany, New York, to serve as a chip factory for U.S. quantum computing firms.

raw.headline: IBM buys HRL Laboratories in shift to two-track quantum computing strategy
raw.summary: IBM has agreed to buy HRL Laboratories, a private quantum computing research lab jointly owned by Boeing Co and General Motors, the ‌company said on Thursday, adding a second pillar to IBM's quantum computing efforts.  IBM has been racing ‌against Alphabet's Google, Microsoft and others to create practical quantum machines that can crack problems that would take conventional computers thousands ​of years to solve.  In May, U.S. President Donald Trump's administration said it would award $1 billion to IBM to set up a new company called Anderon in New Albany, New York, to serve as a chip factory for U.S. quantum computing firms.

headline: IBM to Acquire HRL Laboratories to Power the Future of Quantum
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) today announced it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire HRL Laboratories, LLC (HRL), a flagship research and development institution. HRL is a private company jointly owned by Boeing and General Motors. Both Boeing and GM will continue to partner with IBM on quantum applications and advanced technology development following the transaction.

raw.headline: IBM to Acquire HRL Laboratories to Power the Future of Quantum

headline: Alaska Air (ALK) Adds Cargo Freighters And Replaces Hawaiian Island Fleet
summary: Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) plans to replace Hawaiian Airlines’ Neighbor Island fleet with Boeing 737-800 aircraft. The company is also expanding its cargo operations with additional Boeing Converted Freighters. These operational changes focus on regional passenger service in Hawaii and cargo capacity across Alaska Air’s network. Alaska Air Group sits at the intersection of passenger and cargo air travel, with NYSE:ALK drawing attention whenever it reshapes its fleet. The decision to...
